title: How Great Leaders Inspire Action | Simon Sinek | TED

transcript.chinese: 当事情没有像我们想象的那样发展时，你如何解释？
transcript.english: How do you explain when things don't go as we assume?

transcript.chinese: 或者更好的是，当其他人能够实现似乎违背所有假设的事情时，您如何解释？
transcript.english: Or better, how do you explain when others are able to achieve things that seem to defy all of the assumptions?

transcript.chinese: 例如：为什么苹果如此创新？
transcript.english: For example: Why is Apple so innovative?

transcript.chinese: 年复一年，年复一年，他们比所有竞争对手都更具创新性。
transcript.english: Year after year, after year, they're more innovative than all their competition.

transcript.chinese: 然而，他们只是一家计算机公司。
transcript.english: And yet, they're just a computer company.

transcript.chinese: 他们和其他人一样。
transcript.english: They're just like everyone else.

transcript.chinese: 他们有同样的机会接触到同样的人才、同样的机构、同样的顾问、同样的媒体。
transcript.english: They have the same access to the same talent, the same agencies, the same consultants, the same media.

transcript.chinese: 那为什么他们看起来有些不一样呢？
transcript.english: Then why is it that they seem to have something different?

transcript.chinese: 马丁·路德·金为何领导民权运动？
transcript.english: Why is it that Martin Luther King led the Civil Rights Movement?

transcript.chinese: 他不是唯一一个在民权运动之前的美国遭受苦难的人，他当然也不是当时唯一伟大的演说家。
transcript.english: He wasn't the only man who suffered in pre-civil rights America, and he certainly wasn't the only great orator of the day.

transcript.chinese: 为什么是他？
transcript.english: Why him?

transcript.chinese: 为什么莱特兄弟能够找到受控的动力载人飞行，而当然还有其他团队比他们更有资格、资金更充足——但他们没有实现动力载人飞行，而莱特兄弟却打败了他们。
transcript.english: And why is it that the Wright brothers were able to figure out controlled, powered man flight when there were certainly other teams who were better qualified, better funded -- and they didn't achieve powered man flight, and the Wright brothers beat them to it.

transcript.chinese: 这里还有其他事情在起作用。
transcript.english: There's something else at play here.

transcript.chinese: 大约三年半前，我有了一个发现。
transcript.english: About three and a half years ago, I made a discovery.

transcript.chinese: 这一发现深刻地改变了我对世界运作方式的看法，甚至深刻地改变了我在其中运作的方式。
transcript.english: And this discovery profoundly changed my view on how I thought the world worked, and it even profoundly changed the way in which I operate in it.

transcript.chinese: 事实证明，这是有规律的。
transcript.english: As it turns out, there's a pattern.

transcript.chinese: 事实证明，世界上所有伟大的鼓舞人心的领导者和组织，无论是苹果公司、马丁路德金还是莱特兄弟，他们都以完全相同的方式思考、行动和沟通。
transcript.english: As it turns out, all the great inspiring leaders and organizations in the world, whether it's Apple or Martin Luther King or the Wright brothers, they all think, act and communicate the exact same way.

transcript.chinese: 这与其他人完全相反。
transcript.english: And it's the complete opposite to everyone else.

transcript.chinese: 我所做的只是将其编入法典，这可能是世界上最简单的想法。
transcript.english: All I did was codify it, and it's probably the world's simplest idea.

transcript.chinese: 我称之为黄金圈。
transcript.english: I call it the golden circle.

transcript.chinese: 为什么？
transcript.english: Why?

transcript.chinese: 如何？
transcript.english: How?

transcript.chinese: 什么？
transcript.english: What?

transcript.chinese: 这个小想法解释了为什么一些组织和一些领导者能够激励其他组织和领导者却不能。
transcript.english: This little idea explains why some organizations and some leaders are able to inspire where others aren't.

transcript.chinese: 让我快速定义这些术语。
transcript.english: Let me define the terms really quickly.

transcript.chinese: 地球上的每个人、每个组织都 100% 知道自己在做什么。
transcript.english: Every single person, every single organization on the planet knows what they do, 100 percent.

transcript.chinese: 有些人知道他们是如何做到这一点的，无论你称之为差异化价值主张、专有流程还是你的 USP。
transcript.english: Some know how they do it, whether you call it your differentiated value proposition or your proprietary process or your USP.

transcript.chinese: 但很少有人或组织知道他们为什么要做他们所做的事情。
transcript.english: But very, very few people or organizations know why they do what they do.

transcript.chinese: 我所说的“为什么”并不是“为了盈利”。这就是一个结果。
transcript.english: And by "why" I don't mean "to make a profit." That's a result.

transcript.chinese: 总有一个结果。
transcript.english: It's always a result.

transcript.chinese: 我所说的“为什么”是指：你的目的是什么？
transcript.english: By "why," I mean: What's your purpose?

transcript.chinese: 你的原因是什么？
transcript.english: What's your cause?

transcript.chinese: 你的信仰是什么？
transcript.english: What's your belief?

transcript.chinese: 你的组织为何存在？
transcript.english: Why does your organization exist?

transcript.chinese: 你为什么早上起床？
transcript.english: Why do you get out of bed in the morning?

transcript.chinese: 为什么有人要关心呢？
transcript.english: And why should anyone care?

transcript.chinese: 因此，我们的思考方式、行动方式、沟通方式都是由外而内的，这是显而易见的。
transcript.english: As a result, the way we think, we act, the way we communicate is from the outside in, it's obvious.

transcript.chinese: 我们从最清晰的事物到最模糊的事物。
transcript.english: We go from the clearest thing to the fuzziest thing.

transcript.chinese: 但受启发的领导者和受启发的组织——无论其规模大小，无论其行业如何——都会从内到外地思考、行动和沟通。
transcript.english: But the inspired leaders and the inspired organizations -- regardless of their size, regardless of their industry -- all think, act and communicate from the inside out.

transcript.chinese: 让我举个例子。
transcript.english: Let me give you an example.

transcript.chinese: 我使用苹果是因为它们很容易理解并且每个人都能理解。
transcript.english: I use Apple because they're easy to understand and everybody gets it.

transcript.chinese: 如果苹果公司像其他人一样，他们的营销信息可能听起来像这样：“我们制造出色的电脑。
transcript.english: If Apple were like everyone else, a marketing message from them might sound like this: "We make great computers.

transcript.chinese: 它们设计精美、易于使用且用户友好。
transcript.english: They're beautifully designed, simple to use and user friendly.

transcript.chinese: 想买一台吗？” “嗯。”这就是我们大多数人的沟通方式。
transcript.english: Want to buy one?" "Meh." That's how most of us communicate.

transcript.chinese: 这就是大多数营销和销售的方式，这就是我们人际沟通的方式。
transcript.english: That's how most marketing and sales are done, that's how we communicate interpersonally.

transcript.chinese: 我们说我们做了什么，我们说我们如何与众不同或更好，我们期望某种行为，购买，投票，诸如此类。
transcript.english: We say what we do, we say how we're different or better and we expect some sort of a behavior, a purchase, a vote, something like that.

transcript.chinese: 这是我们的新律师事务所：我们拥有最好的律师和最大的客户，我们始终为客户服务。
transcript.english: Here's our new law firm: We have the best lawyers with the biggest clients, we always perform for our clients.

transcript.chinese: 这是我们的新车：它的油耗很高，有真皮座椅。
transcript.english: Here's our new car: It gets great gas mileage, it has leather seats.

transcript.chinese: 买我们的车。
transcript.english: Buy our car.

transcript.chinese: 但这并不鼓舞人心。
transcript.english: But it's uninspiring.

transcript.chinese: 苹果实际上是这样沟通的。
transcript.english: Here's how Apple actually communicates.

transcript.chinese: “我们所做的一切都相信挑战现状。
transcript.english: "Everything we do, we believe in challenging the status quo.

transcript.chinese: 我们相信不同的思维方式。
transcript.english: We believe in thinking differently.

transcript.chinese: 我们挑战现状的方式是让我们的产品设计精美、使用简单且用户友好。
transcript.english: The way we challenge the status quo is by making our products beautifully designed, simple to use and user friendly.

transcript.chinese: 我们只是碰巧制造了出色的计算机。
transcript.english: We just happen to make great computers.

transcript.chinese: 想买一台吗？”
transcript.english: Want to buy one?"

transcript.chinese: 完全不同，对吧？
transcript.english: Totally different, right?

transcript.chinese: 你准备好从我这里购买一台电脑了。
transcript.english: You're ready to buy a computer from me.

transcript.chinese: 我只是颠倒了信息的顺序。
transcript.english: I just reversed the order of the information.

transcript.chinese: 它向我们证明的是，人们不会购买你所做的事情，而是会购买你所做的事情。人们会购买你这样做的原因。
transcript.english: What it proves to us is that people don't buy what you do; people buy why you do it.

transcript.chinese: 这就解释了为什么在座的每个人都非常乐意购买苹果电脑。
transcript.english: This explains why every single person in this room is perfectly comfortable buying a computer from Apple.

transcript.chinese: 但我们也非常乐意购买 Apple 的 MP3 播放器、Apple 的手机或 Apple 的 DVR。
transcript.english: But we're also perfectly comfortable buying an MP3 player from Apple, or a phone from Apple, or a DVR from Apple.

transcript.chinese: 正如我之前所说，苹果只是一家电脑公司。
transcript.english: As I said before, Apple's just a computer company.

transcript.chinese: 从结构上看，他们与任何竞争对手都没有什么区别。
transcript.english: Nothing distinguishes them structurally from any of their competitors.

transcript.chinese: 他们的竞争对手同样有资格生产所有这些产品。
transcript.english: Their competitors are equally qualified to make all of these products.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，他们尝试过。
transcript.english: In fact, they tried.

transcript.chinese: 几年前，Gateway 推出了平板电视。
transcript.english: A few years ago, Gateway came out with flat-screen TVs.

transcript.chinese: 他们非常有资格制造平板电视。
transcript.english: They're eminently qualified to make flat-screen TVs.

transcript.chinese: 他们多年来一直生产平面显示器。
transcript.english: They've been making flat-screen monitors for years.

transcript.chinese: 没人买。
transcript.english: Nobody bought one.

transcript.chinese: 戴尔推出了 MP3 播放器和 PDA，他们生产优质的产品，而且他们可以生产设计完美的产品——但没有人买。
transcript.english: Dell came out with MP3 players and PDAs, and they make great quality products, and they can make perfectly well-designed products -- and nobody bought one.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，现在说起来，我们甚至无法想象从戴尔购买MP3播放器。
transcript.english: In fact, talking about it now, we can't even imagine buying an MP3 player from Dell.

transcript.chinese: 您为什么要从计算机公司购买一台？
transcript.english: Why would you buy one from a computer company?

transcript.chinese: 但我们每天都这样做。
transcript.english: But we do it every day.

transcript.chinese: 人们不买你做的事，而是买你做的事。他们购买你这样做的理由。
transcript.english: People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it.

transcript.chinese: 我们的目标不是与每个需要您所拥有的东西的人做生意。
transcript.english: The goal is not to do business with everybody who needs what you have.

transcript.chinese: 我们的目标是与那些相信你的信念的人做生意。
transcript.english: The goal is to do business with people who believe what you believe.

transcript.chinese: 这是最好的部分：我告诉你的一切都不是我的观点。
transcript.english: Here's the best part: None of what I'm telling you is my opinion.

transcript.chinese: 这一切都基于生物学原理。
transcript.english: It's all grounded in the tenets of biology.

transcript.chinese: 不是心理学，是生物学。
transcript.english: Not psychology, biology.

transcript.chinese: 如果你看一下人脑的横截面，从上到下，人脑实际上分为三个主要部分，与黄金圈完美相关。
transcript.english: If you look at a cross-section of the human brain, from the top down, the human brain is actually broken into three major components that correlate perfectly with the golden circle.

transcript.chinese: 我们最新的大脑，我们的智人大脑，我们的新皮质，对应于“什么”层面。
transcript.english: Our newest brain, our Homo sapien brain, our neocortex, corresponds with the "what" level.

transcript.chinese: 新皮质负责我们所有的理性和分析性思维和语言。
transcript.english: The neocortex is responsible for all of our rational and analytical thought and language.

transcript.chinese: 中间的两个部分组成了我们的边缘脑，我们的边缘脑负责我们所有的感觉，比如信任和忠诚。
transcript.english: The middle two sections make up our limbic brains, and our limbic brains are responsible for all of our feelings, like trust and loyalty.

transcript.chinese: 它还负责所有人类行为、所有决策，但它没有语言能力。
transcript.english: It's also responsible for all human behavior, all decision-making, and it has no capacity for language.

transcript.chinese: 换句话说，当我们从外向内沟通时，是的，人们可以理解大量复杂的信息，例如功能、优点、事实和数据。
transcript.english: In other words, when we communicate from the outside in, yes, people can understand vast amounts of complicated information like features and benefits and facts and figures.

transcript.chinese: 它只是不会驱动行为。
transcript.english: It just doesn't drive behavior.

transcript.chinese: 当我们能够由内而外地沟通时，我们就直接与大脑中控制行为的部分交谈，然后我们让人们用我们所说和所做的有形的事情来合理化它。
transcript.english: When we can communicate from the inside out, we're talking directly to the part of the brain that controls behavior, and then we allow people to rationalize it with the tangible things we say and do.

transcript.chinese: 这就是直觉决定的来源。
transcript.english: This is where gut decisions come from.

transcript.chinese: 有时，你可以向某人提供所有事实和数据，他们会说：“我知道所有事实和细节的意思，但就是感觉不对。”为什么我们要使用那个动词，它“感觉”不对？
transcript.english: Sometimes you can give somebody all the facts and figures, and they say, "I know what all the facts and details say, but it just doesn't feel right." Why would we use that verb, it doesn't "feel" right?

transcript.chinese: 因为大脑中控制决策的部分并不控制语言。
transcript.english: Because the part of the brain that controls decision-making doesn't control language.

transcript.chinese: 我们最多只能说：“我不知道。
transcript.english: The best we can muster up is, "I don't know.

transcript.chinese: 就是感觉不对。”或者有时你说你用你的心或灵魂来领导。
transcript.english: It just doesn't feel right." Or sometimes you say you're leading with your heart or soul.

transcript.chinese: 我不想告诉你，那些不是控制你行为的其他身体部位。
transcript.english: I hate to break it to you, those aren't other body parts controlling your behavior.

transcript.chinese: 这一切都发生在你的边缘脑中，这是大脑中控制决策而不是语言的部分。
transcript.english: It's all happening here in your limbic brain, the part of the brain that controls decision-making and not language.

transcript.chinese: 但如果你不知道你为什么做你所做的事情，而人们对你为什么做你所做的事情做出反应，那么你如何让人们投票给你，或者从你那里购买东西，或者更重要的是，忠诚并希望成为你所做的事情的一部分。
transcript.english: But if you don't know why you do what you do, and people respond to why you do what you do, then how will you ever get people to vote for you, or buy something from you, or, more importantly, be loyal and want to be a part of what it is that you do.

transcript.chinese: 我们的目标不仅仅是把你所拥有的产品卖给需要的人；目标是把产品卖给那些和你有同样信念的人。
transcript.english: The goal is not just to sell to people who need what you have; the goal is to sell to people who believe what you believe.

transcript.chinese: 我们的目标不仅仅是雇佣需要工作的人；就是雇用那些相信你所相信的人。
transcript.english: The goal is not just to hire people who need a job; it's to hire people who believe what you believe.

transcript.chinese: 我总是说，你知道，如果你雇佣员工只是因为他们能胜任工作，他们就会为你的钱而工作，但如果他们相信你所相信的，他们就会用血、汗和泪水为你工作。
transcript.english: I always say that, you know, if you hire people just because they can do a job, they'll work for your money, but if they believe what you believe, they'll work for you with blood and sweat and tears.

transcript.chinese: 没有比莱特兄弟更好的例子了。
transcript.english: Nowhere else is there a better example than with the Wright brothers.

transcript.chinese: 大多数人不知道塞缪尔·皮尔庞特·兰利。
transcript.english: Most people don't know about Samuel Pierpont Langley.

transcript.chinese: 早在 20 世纪初，对载人动力飞行的追求就像当时的互联网一样。
transcript.english: And back in the early 20th century, the pursuit of powered man flight was like the dot com of the day.

transcript.chinese: 每个人都在尝试。
transcript.english: Everybody was trying it.

transcript.chinese: 我们认为，塞缪尔·皮尔庞特·兰利是成功的秘诀。
transcript.english: And Samuel Pierpont Langley had, what we assume, to be the recipe for success.

transcript.chinese: 即使是现在，你也会问人们：“为什么你的产品或者你的公司失败了？”人们总是给你同样的三件事的相同排列：资本不足、错误的人、糟糕的市场状况。
transcript.english: Even now, you ask people, "Why did your product or why did your company fail?" and people always give you the same permutation of the same three things: under-capitalized, the wrong people, bad market conditions.

transcript.chinese: 这三件事总是相同的，所以让我们来探讨一下。
transcript.english: It's always the same three things, so let's explore that.

transcript.chinese: 美国陆军部拨款塞缪尔·皮尔庞特·兰利 5 万美元来研发这种飞行器。
transcript.english: Samuel Pierpont Langley was given 50,000 dollars by the War Department to figure out this flying machine.

transcript.chinese: 钱没问题。
transcript.english: Money was no problem.

transcript.chinese: 他曾在哈佛大学任职，并在史密森学会工作，人脉非常广。他了解当时所有的大人物。
transcript.english: He held a seat at Harvard and worked at the Smithsonian and was extremely well-connected; he knew all the big minds of the day.

transcript.chinese: 他聘请了金钱能找到的最优秀的人才，而且市场条件非常好。
transcript.english: He hired the best minds money could find and the market conditions were fantastic.

transcript.chinese: 《纽约时报》到处跟踪他，每个人都支持兰利。
transcript.english: The New York Times followed him around everywhere, and everyone was rooting for Langley.

transcript.chinese: 那我们为什么从未听说过塞缪尔·皮尔庞特·兰利呢？
transcript.english: Then how come we've never heard of Samuel Pierpont Langley?

transcript.chinese: 几百英里外的俄亥俄州代顿市的奥维尔·赖特和威尔伯·赖特夫妇没有任何我们认为的成功秘诀。
transcript.english: A few hundred miles away in Dayton, Ohio, Orville and Wilbur Wright, they had none of what we consider to be the recipe for success.

transcript.chinese: 他们没有钱；他们用自行车店的收益来实现自己的梦想。
transcript.english: They had no money; they paid for their dream with the proceeds from their bicycle shop.

transcript.chinese: 莱特兄弟团队中没有一个人受过大学教育，甚至奥维尔和威尔伯也没有。
transcript.english: Not a single person on the Wright brothers' team had a college education, not even Orville or Wilbur.

transcript.chinese: 《纽约时报》也无处追踪他们。
transcript.english: And The New York Times followed them around nowhere.

transcript.chinese: 不同之处在于，奥维尔和威尔伯是被一个事业、一个目的、一个信念所驱动。
transcript.english: The difference was, Orville and Wilbur were driven by a cause, by a purpose, by a belief.

transcript.chinese: 他们相信，如果他们能研制出这种飞行器，它将改变世界的进程。
transcript.english: They believed that if they could figure out this flying machine, it'll change the course of the world.

transcript.chinese: 塞缪尔·皮尔庞特·兰利则不同。
transcript.english: Samuel Pierpont Langley was different.

transcript.chinese: 他想要变得富有，他想要出名。
transcript.english: He wanted to be rich, and he wanted to be famous.

transcript.chinese: 他追求的是结果。
transcript.english: He was in pursuit of the result.

transcript.chinese: 他追求的是财富。
transcript.english: He was in pursuit of the riches.

transcript.chinese: 你瞧，看看发生了什么。
transcript.english: And lo and behold, look what happened.

transcript.chinese: 相信莱特兄弟梦想的人们与他们一起付出了血汗和泪水。
transcript.english: The people who believed in the Wright brothers' dream worked with them with blood and sweat and tears.

transcript.chinese: 其他人只是为了薪水而工作。
transcript.english: The others just worked for the paycheck.

transcript.chinese: 他们讲述了莱特兄弟每次外出时都必须带上五套零件的故事，因为这就是他们晚饭前坠机的次数。
transcript.english: They tell stories of how every time the Wright brothers went out, they would have to take five sets of parts, because that's how many times they would crash before supper.

transcript.chinese: 最终，1903 年 12 月 17 日，莱特兄弟成功起飞，但没有人亲眼目睹。
transcript.english: And, eventually, on December 17th, 1903, the Wright brothers took flight, and no one was there to even experience it.

transcript.chinese: 几天后我们才知道这件事。
transcript.english: We found out about it a few days later.

transcript.chinese: 进一步证明兰利的动机是错误的：莱特兄弟起飞的那天，他退出了。
transcript.english: And further proof that Langley was motivated by the wrong thing: the day the Wright brothers took flight, he quit.

transcript.chinese: 他本可以说：“伙计们，这是一个了不起的发现，我将改进你们的技术，”但他没有。
transcript.english: He could have said, "That's an amazing discovery, guys, and I will improve upon your technology," but he didn't.

transcript.chinese: 他不是第一，他没有致富，他没有成名，所以他退出了。
transcript.english: He wasn't first, he didn't get rich, he didn't get famous, so he quit.

transcript.chinese: 人们不买你做的事，而是买你做的事。他们购买你这样做的理由。
transcript.english: People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it.

transcript.chinese: 如果你谈论你的信仰，你就会吸引那些相信你信仰的人。
transcript.english: If you talk about what you believe, you will attract those who believe what you believe.

transcript.chinese: 但为什么吸引那些相信你所信仰的人很重要呢？
transcript.english: But why is it important to attract those who believe what you believe?

transcript.chinese: 有一种叫做创新扩散定律的东西，如果你不知道这个定律，你就知道术语。
transcript.english: Something called the law of diffusion of innovation, if you don't know the law, you know the terminology.

transcript.chinese: 我们人口中的前 2.5% 是我们的创新者。
transcript.english: The first 2.5% of our population are our innovators.

transcript.chinese: 接下来 13.5% 的人口是我们的早期采用者。
transcript.english: The next 13.5% of our population are our early adopters.

transcript.chinese: 接下来的 34% 是早期多数、晚期多数和落后者。
transcript.english: The next 34% are your early majority, your late majority and your laggards.

transcript.chinese: 这些人购买按键式电话的唯一原因是因为你不能再购买旋转式电话了。
transcript.english: The only reason these people buy touch-tone phones is because you can't buy rotary phones anymore.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）在这个规模上，我们在不同的时间坐在不同的地方，但是创新扩散定律告诉我们，如果你想要大众市场的成功或大众市场对一个想法的接受，你就无法拥有它，直到你达到 15% 到 18% 的市场渗透率之间的临界点，
transcript.english: (Laughter) We all sit at various places at various times on this scale, but what the law of diffusion of innovation tells us is that if you want mass-market success or mass-market acceptance of an idea, you cannot have it until you achieve this tipping point between 15 and 18 percent market penetration,

transcript.chinese: 然后系统提示。
transcript.english: and then the system tips.

transcript.chinese: 我喜欢问企业：“你们的新业务转化率是多少？”他们喜欢自豪地告诉你，“大约是百分之十”。
transcript.english: I love asking businesses, "What's your conversion on new business?" They love to tell you, "It's about 10 percent," proudly.

transcript.chinese: 嗯，你可能会绊倒超过 10% 的顾客。
transcript.english: Well, you can trip over 10% of the customers.

transcript.chinese: 我们都有大约 10% 的人只是“明白了”。我们就是这样描述它们的，对吧？
transcript.english: We all have about 10% who just "get it." That's how we describe them, right?

transcript.chinese: 这就像直觉，“哦，他们明白了。”问题是：如何找到在开展业务之前就获得了它的人和没有获得它的人？
transcript.english: That's like that gut feeling, "Oh, they just get it." The problem is: How do you find the ones that get it before doing business versus the ones who don't get it?

transcript.chinese: 所以，这就是你必须弥合的这个小差距，正如杰弗里摩尔所说，“跨越鸿沟”——因为，你看，早期的大多数人不会尝试某件事，除非其他人先尝试过。
transcript.english: So it's this here, this little gap that you have to close, as Jeffrey Moore calls it, "Crossing the Chasm" -- because, you see, the early majority will not try something until someone else has tried it first.

transcript.chinese: 这些人，创新者和早期采用者，他们很乐意做出这些直觉决定。
transcript.english: And these guys, the innovators and the early adopters, they're comfortable making those gut decisions.

transcript.chinese: 他们更愿意根据自己对世界的看法而做出直观的决定，而不仅仅是可用的产品。
transcript.english: They're more comfortable making those intuitive decisions that are driven by what they believe about the world and not just what product is available.

transcript.chinese: 这些人在 iPhone 刚上市时就排了六个小时的队购买 iPhone，而你本可以在下周就买到现成的 iPhone。
transcript.english: These are the people who stood in line for six hours to buy an iPhone when they first came out, when you could have bought one off the shelf the next week.

transcript.chinese: 这些人在平板电视刚问世时就花了 4 万美元购买，尽管技术并不合格。
transcript.english: These are the people who spent 40,000 dollars on flat-screen TVs when they first came out, even though the technology was substandard.

transcript.chinese: 顺便说一句，他们这样做并不是因为技术太先进了；而是因为技术太先进了。他们是为自己做的。
transcript.english: And, by the way, they didn't do it because the technology was so great; they did it for themselves.

transcript.chinese: 这是因为他们想成为第一。
transcript.english: It's because they wanted to be first.

transcript.chinese: 人们不买你做的事，而是买你做的事。他们相信你这样做的原因，而你所做的只是证明了你的信念。
transcript.english: People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it and what you do simply proves what you believe.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，人们会做一些事情来证明他们所相信的事情。
transcript.english: In fact, people will do the things that prove what they believe.

transcript.chinese: 人们之所以在前六个小时内购买 iPhone，并排了六个小时的队，是因为他们对世界的信念，以及他们希望每个人如何看待他们：他们是第一。
transcript.english: The reason that person bought the iPhone in the first six hours, stood in line for six hours, was because of what they believed about the world, and how they wanted everybody to see them: they were first.

transcript.chinese: 人们不买你做的事，而是买你做的事。他们购买你这样做的理由。
transcript.english: People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it.

transcript.chinese: 那么，让我给你们举一个著名的例子，创新扩散定律的一个著名的失败和一个著名的成功。
transcript.english: So let me give you a famous example, a famous failure and a famous success of the law of diffusion of innovation.

transcript.chinese: 首先是著名的失败。
transcript.english: First, the famous failure.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个商业示例。
transcript.english: It's a commercial example.

transcript.chinese: 正如我们之前所说，成功的秘诀是金钱、合适的人才和合适的市场条件。
transcript.english: As we said before, the recipe for success is money and the right people and the right market conditions.

transcript.chinese: 那你应该会成功。
transcript.english: You should have success then.

transcript.chinese: 看看蒂沃。
transcript.english: Look at TiVo.

transcript.chinese: 从大约八九年前TiVo问世到今天，它们都是市场上质量最高的产品，毫无争议。
transcript.english: From the time TiVo came out about eight or nine years ago to this current day, they are the single highest-quality product on the market, hands down, there is no dispute.

transcript.chinese: 他们的资金非常充足。
transcript.english: They were extremely well-funded.

transcript.chinese: 市场状况非常好。
transcript.english: Market conditions were fantastic.

transcript.chinese: 我的意思是，我们使用 TiVo 作为动词。
transcript.english: I mean, we use TiVo as verb.

transcript.chinese: 我一直在我的垃圾时代华纳 DVR 上播放 TiVo 内容。
transcript.english: I TiVo stuff on my piece-of-junk Time Warner DVR all the time.

transcript.chinese: （笑声）但是 TiVo 在商业上是失败的。
transcript.english: (Laughter) But TiVo's a commercial failure.

transcript.chinese: 他们从来没有赚过钱。
transcript.english: They've never made money.

transcript.chinese: 当他们进行 IPO 时，他们的股票价格约为 30 或 40 美元，然后暴跌，并且交易价格从未超过 10 美元。事实上，我认为除了一些小峰值之外，它的交易量甚至没有超过六点。
transcript.english: And when they went IPO, their stock was at about 30 or 40 dollars and then plummeted, and it's never traded above 10.

transcript.chinese: 因为你看，当 TiVo 推出他们的产品时，他们告诉我们他们拥有什么。
transcript.english: In fact, I don't think it's even traded above six, except for a couple of little spikes.

transcript.chinese: 他们说：“我们有一款产品可以暂停直播电视、跳过广告、倒带直播电视并记住您的观看习惯，甚至无需您询问。”
transcript.english: Because you see, when TiVo launched their product, they told us all what they had.

transcript.chinese: 愤世嫉俗的大多数人说：“我们不相信你。
transcript.english: They said, "We have a product that pauses live TV, skips commercials, rewinds live TV and memorizes your viewing habits without you even asking."

transcript.chinese: 我们不需要它。
transcript.english: And the cynical majority said, "We don't believe you.

transcript.chinese: 我们不喜欢它。
transcript.english: We don't need it.

transcript.chinese: 你吓到我们了。”
transcript.english: We don't like it.

transcript.chinese: 如果他们说：“如果你是那种喜欢完全控制生活各个方面的人，孩子，我们有一款适合你的产品吗？它可以暂停电视直播、跳过广告、记住你的观看习惯等等。”人们不买你做的事，而是买你做的事。他们购买你这样做的理由，而你所做的只是证明你的信念。
transcript.english: You're scaring us."

transcript.chinese: 现在让我给大家举一个创新扩散定律的成功例子。
transcript.english: What if they had said, "If you're the kind of person who likes to have total control over every aspect of your life, boy, do we have a product for you.

transcript.chinese: 1963 年夏天，25 万人出现在华盛顿的购物中心聆听
transcript.english: It pauses live TV, skips commercials, memorizes your viewing habits, etc., etc." People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it, and what you do simply serves as the proof of what you believe.

transcript.chinese: 博士的演讲。国王说话。
transcript.english: Now let me give you a successful example of the law of diffusion of innovation.

transcript.chinese: 他们没有发出邀请，也没有网站可以查询日期。
transcript.english: In the summer of 1963, 250,000 people showed up on the mall in Washington to hear Dr.

transcript.chinese: 你是怎么做到的？
transcript.english: King speak.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，博士
transcript.english: They sent out no invitations, and there was no website to check the date.

transcript.chinese: 金并不是美国唯一一位伟大的演说家。
transcript.english: How do you do that?

transcript.chinese: 他并不是唯一一个在民权运动前的美国遭受苦难的人。
transcript.english: Well, Dr.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，他的一些想法是不好的。
transcript.english: King wasn't the only man in America who was a great orator.

transcript.chinese: 但他有天赋。
transcript.english: He wasn't the only man in America who suffered in a pre-civil rights America.

transcript.chinese: 他没有到处告诉人们美国需要改变什么。
transcript.english: In fact, some of his ideas were bad.

transcript.chinese: 他四处走动，告诉人们他的信仰。
transcript.english: But he had a gift.

transcript.chinese: “我相信，我相信，我相信，”他告诉人们。
transcript.english: He didn't go around telling people what needed to change in America.

transcript.chinese: 那些相信他所信仰的人接受了他的事业，并将其变成自己的事业，并告诉人们。
transcript.english: He went around and told people what he believed.

transcript.chinese: 其中一些人创建了一些结构来向更多人传播信息。
transcript.english: "I believe, I believe, I believe," he told people.

transcript.chinese: 你瞧，有 250,000 人在正确的日子、正确的时间出现聆听他的演讲。
transcript.english: And people who believed what he believed took his cause, and they made it their own, and they told people.

transcript.chinese: 有多少人为他出现？
transcript.english: And some of those people created structures to get the word out to even more people.

transcript.chinese: 零。
transcript.english: And lo and behold, 250,000 people showed up on the right day at the right time to hear him speak.

transcript.chinese: 他们为自己而出现。
transcript.english: How many of them showed up for him?

transcript.chinese: 正是他们对美国的信念让他们在八月中旬乘坐公共汽车八个小时来到华盛顿站在阳光下。
transcript.english: Zero.

transcript.chinese: 这就是他们所相信的，而且这与黑人与白人无关：25% 的观众是白人。
transcript.english: They showed up for themselves.

transcript.chinese: 博士
transcript.english: It's what they believed about America that got them to travel in a bus for eight hours to stand in the sun in Washington in the middle of August.

transcript.chinese: 金认为，这个世界上有两种法律：由更高权威制定的法律和由人制定的法律。
transcript.english: It's what they believed, and it wasn't about black versus white: 25% of the audience was white.

transcript.chinese: 只有当人类制定的所有法律都与更高权威制定的法律一致时，我们才会生活在一个公正的世界中。
transcript.english: Dr.

transcript.chinese: 碰巧的是，民权运动是帮助他实现自己事业的最佳选择。
transcript.english: King believed that there are two types of laws in this world: those that are made by a higher authority and those that are made by men.

transcript.chinese: 我们跟随，不是为了他，而是为了我们自己。
transcript.english: And not until all the laws that are made by men are consistent with the laws made by the higher authority will we live in a just world.

transcript.chinese: 顺便说一句，他发表的是“我有一个梦想”的演讲，而不是“我有一个计划”的演讲。
transcript.english: It just so happened that the Civil Rights Movement was the perfect thing to help him bring his cause to life.

transcript.chinese: （笑声） 现在听听政客们的意见，他们有全面的 12 点计划。
transcript.english: We followed, not for him, but for ourselves.

transcript.chinese: 他们没有激励任何人。
transcript.english: By the way, he gave the "I have a dream" speech, not the "I have a plan" speech.

transcript.chinese: 因为有领导者，也有领导者。
transcript.english: (Laughter) Listen to politicians now, with their comprehensive 12-point plans.

transcript.chinese: 领导者拥有权力或权威，但领导者会激励我们。
transcript.english: They're not inspiring anybody.

transcript.chinese: 无论他们是个人还是组织，我们都会追随那些领导者，不是因为我们必须这样做，而是因为我们愿意这样做。
transcript.english: Because there are leaders and there are those who lead.

transcript.chinese: 我们追随那些领导者，不是为了他们，而是为了我们自己。
transcript.english: Leaders hold a position of power or authority, but those who lead inspire us.

transcript.chinese: 那些从“为什么”开始的人有能力激励周围的人或找到其他激励他们的人。
transcript.english: Whether they're individuals or organizations, we follow those who lead, not because we have to, but because we want to.

transcript.chinese: 非常感谢。
transcript.english: We follow those who lead, not for them, but for ourselves.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）
transcript.english: And it's those who start with "why" that have the ability to inspire those around them or find others who inspire them.

transcript.chinese: 非常感谢。
transcript.english: Thank you very much.

transcript.chinese: （掌声）
transcript.english: (Applause)
